title: feat: tag virtual makeup results
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
- Publishes generated makeup images with `virtual-makeup`
- Keeps uploaded face references untagged
- Falls back to the generated blob if publishing fails

Checks: Biome, esbuild, source/output tagging contract

gist.headline: Makeup gets its own pollen trail
gist.blurb: Virtual Makeup results now leave the garden wearing a proper `virtual-makeup` tag. Uploaded face references stay private to the workbench, as they should.
gist.summary: The Virtual Makeup app now publishes generated makeup images with the `virtual-makeup` tag, keeps uploaded face references untagged, and returns the generated blob if publishing is unavailable.
gist.impact: People using Virtual Makeup get shareable, correctly labeled result URLs while their original face-reference uploads remain untagged and generation still completes when publishing fails.
gist.visual_concept: Depict the Virtual Makeup app as a cozy vanity workstation: Polly wears a tiny makeup artist beret and carefully stamps a finished portrait with a `virtual-makeup` label, while the original reference photo rests safely in an unmarked drawer. A small code-plant and flowers suggest organized, resilient publishing, with the finished image glowing from a retro monitor.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly, the cute yellow-and-brown striped bee mascot with pink cheek blush and light blue wings, wearing a tiny makeup artist beret as she stamps a glowing generated portrait on a retro vanity monitor with simple pixel text: "virtual-makeup"; an untagged reference photo is tucked safely into a small drawer beside her. Lime green (#ecf874) appears prominently in the monitor glow and flower pots, with code-plants, pastel lavender and warm cream surroundings, cozy pixel art, 8-bit aesthetic, soft pastel gradients, warm lighting, retro gaming vibes, nostalgic but beautiful.
